heroSubtitle: The first interactive K-5 Arabic learning platform for families and schools

howItWorksTitle: Learning Arabic has never been easier
howItWorksSubtitle: Three simple steps to get started.
step1Desc: Create an account and your child starts learning immediately.
step2Desc: Interactive lessons with audio, matching games, and instant feedback.
step3Desc: Track progress, earn badges, and celebrate milestones together.
feature1Desc: Every letter and word comes with native Arabic pronunciation. Kids learn to speak, not just read.
feature2Desc: Earn culturally meaningful badges and achievements as you progress through the curriculum.
feature3Desc: Ta3allam doesn't just teach on screens — it inspires kids to pick up a pencil and practice. Digital lessons build the foundation, real writing makes it stick.
curriculumSubtitle: Structured learning from the Arabic alphabet to reading comprehension and cultural awareness.
kindergartenDesc: Arabic alphabet, basic vocabulary, simple expressions
grade1Desc: Reading skills, expanded vocabulary, simple sentences
grade2Desc: Reading comprehension, grammar basics, cultural awareness
grade3Desc: Intermediate vocabulary, verb conjugation, Arabic history
grade4Desc: Advanced reading, complex grammar, Arabic literature
grade5Desc: Fluency building, essay writing, cultural projects
schoolsSubtitle: Give your teachers the tools to teach Arabic effectively.
schoolFeature1Desc: Monitor student progress, manage classes, customize content.
schoolFeature2Desc: Join codes, student rosters, automatic grade-level assignment.
schoolFeature3Desc: Starting at $3/student/year. Contact us for a school package.
whyDesc: Arabic-speaking families in the diaspora face a common challenge — keeping their children connected to their heritage language. Between school, homework, and activities, there's never enough time for Arabic. Ta3allam was built to solve this. A structured, interactive Arabic curriculum that works in just 5 minutes a day. Designed with Arabic language educators, every lesson builds on the last — from the alphabet through reading comprehension. No textbooks, no scheduling conflicts. Just open it up, learn together, and watch your child's Arabic grow.
diff1Desc: Not a translation. Purpose-built curriculum designed for how Arabic is taught in diaspora schools and communities.
diff2Desc: Earn culturally meaningful badges. Learn through content that connects to Arabic heritage and traditions.
diff3Desc: 5 minutes a day is all it takes. Works on any device — phone, tablet, or laptop. No scheduling, no driving to lessons.
pricingSubtitle: Our complete curriculum has no cost. Support us to help more families.
planPaidPeriod: Help keep it accessible for everyone
faq1A: Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) — the standard used across the Arab world for education, media, and formal communication.
faq2A: Kindergarten through Grade 5. We're adding more levels regularly.
faq3Q: Do I need to speak Arabic to help my child?
faq3A: No! The platform guides your child through each lesson. English hints are available when needed.
faq4A: Absolutely. No ads, no social features, no external links. Your child only sees learning content. COPPA compliant.
faq5A: Our curriculum is designed for Arabic learners in the diaspora and structured to build progressively from the alphabet through reading comprehension. Content is reviewed by Arabic language educators for accuracy.
faq6A: Yes! We offer school plans with teacher dashboards and class management. Contact us at schools@ta3allam.org.
faq7Q: How long does each lesson take?
faq7A: About 5 minutes. Short enough to fit into any daily routine, long enough to make real progress.
faq8Q: How do I keep my child focused on Ta3allam?
faq8A: Use your device's built-in focus mode! On iPad or iPhone, turn on Guided Access (Settings → Accessibility → Guided Access). Once enabled, triple-click the side button while Ta3allam is open — your child won't be able to leave the app until you unlock it. On Android, use Screen Pinning (Settings → Security). These features are designed exactly for this and work better than anything we could build into a website.
ctaTitle: Your child's Arabic journey starts today
ctaSubtitle: Join families across the diaspora who are keeping the language alive.
